description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the node:10 Docker image with a node_modules/ cache and stages test, post_test, build, deploy, and post_deploy. In the test stage add a test job that curls the Google Cloud SDK installer, sources it, writes $CLOUDSTORAGE_TEST_KEY_FILE to a temporary JSON file, authenticates with gcloud, runs a script to download test environment variables, executes npm ci, npm run lint, and npm run test:report (producing reports/unit_test/report.xml as a JUnit artifact and applying a coverage regex), while using a mongo:3.6 service and setting GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS, MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_PASSWORD, and MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_USERNAME variables; also add dockerfile_lint and shellcheck_lint jobs that use docker:latest with docker:dind, running hadolint on the Dockerfile and koalaman/shellcheck on all *.sh scripts respectively. In the build stage add build_bitbucket_dashboard that clones https://github.com/sweetim/bitbucket_pipeline_dashboard, runs npm install and npm run build, and stores bitbucket_pipeline_dashboard/dist/ as an artifact, and build_nuxt that runs npm ci and npm run build, publishing dist/ and static/ as artifacts. In the deploy stage add deploy_image_to_dockerhub (docker login with $DOCKERHUB_USER/$DOCKERHUB_PASSWORD, docker build -t timx/timx-site ., docker push timx/timx-site) and deploy_image_to_gitlab_registry (docker login with gitlab-ci-token and $CI_JOB_TOKEN to registry.gitlab.com, docker build -t registry.gitlab.com/hosweetim/timx-site ., docker push that image), both restricted to the master branch and using docker:dind, plus a deploy_production job that depends on the two build jobs, moves the dashboard dist into static/bitbucket, authenticates with $DEPLOY_KEY_FILE_PRODUCTION, downloads production env variables, and runs gcloud app deploy; in post_deploy add clean_up_gae_versions that authenticates with $DEPLOY_KEY_FILE_PRODUCTION and runs scripts/clean_up_gae.sh 5, also only on master. Finally, include a manual generate_release_note job that runs ./scripts/generate_release.sh with CI variables, and a post_test upload_to_sonarqube job that runs npm ci and npm run sonar-scanner only on master.
